# Metro Integrated Planning System - Progress Log

## Current Task: DESIGN-REVISION - Critical Document Revision

### Status: ANALYSIS COMPLETE - Ready for Revision

---

## Session: 2025-12-20 (Harness Analysis & Recovery)

### Task: Analyze context loss and improve harness protocol

**Objective:** Analyze last 2 commits and Claude logs to understand what was done correctly, hallucinations, and context loss.

### Findings

#### 1. Context Loss Analysis

| Metric | Before | After | Lost |
|--------|--------|-------|------|
| claude-progress.txt | 2338 lines | 205 lines | **91%** |
| features.json | 392 lines | 142 lines | **64%** |

**Root Cause:** Sessions saved to harness only at the end, not incrementally.

#### 2. Hallucinations in DESIGN_SPEC.md

| Issue | Expected | Found |
|-------|----------|-------|
| Tech Stack | Vite + React 18 | "Astro.js 4.x" |
| References | Timeline/Gantt examples | Geospatial maps (irrelevant) |
| Sandbox Mode | Detailed state machine | Vague description |

#### 3. What Was Done Correctly

- вң… Created DESIGN_SPEC.md (~1500 lines)
- вң… Identified critical issues in analysis
- вң… Saved plan to `.claude/plans/elegant-sparking-blossom.md`
- вң… Created backup in `docs/archive/`
- вң… Identified new reference sources (Epicflow, Dime Scheduler, AVS)

#### 4. POCs That Exist (from backup)

| POC | Location | Status |
|-----|----------|--------|
| XState Viewport | `stories/01-POCs/State/XStateViewport.stories.tsx` | вң… |
| Semantic Zoom | `stories/01-POCs/Rendering/SemanticZoom.stories.tsx` | вң… |
| Time Scrubber (7 variants) | `stories/01-POCs/TimeScrubber/` | вң… |
| deck.gl Heatmap | `stories/01-POCs/Rendering/` | вң… |
| FractalCanvas | `stories/FractalCanvas*.tsx` | вң… |

### New Harness Rules Added

1. Save BEFORE starting any task
2. Save AFTER each subtask
3. Checkpoint every 15-20 minutes
4. Save before intensive operations
5. If context > 70%, STOP and save

### Anthropic Harness Research (Completed)

Source: [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)

**Key Learnings Applied:**
- JSON preferred for state files (less likely to be overwritten)
- Failure pattern 1: Doing too much вҶ’ context exhaustion
- Failure pattern 2: Declaring done prematurely
- Incremental commits enable recovery
- Session startup rituals: always read context before acting

### Next Steps

1. Continue with DESIGN-REVISION task
2. Fetch new reference screenshots
3. Correct tech stack in DESIGN_SPEC.md

**Status:** Harness protocol improved вң…

---

## Session: 2025-12-19 (Critical Analysis)

### Executive Summary

The DESIGN_SPEC.md document has **CRITICAL issues** that must be resolved before implementation. A thorough analysis was completed and new reference sources were identified by the user.

---

## рҹ”ҙ CRITICAL ISSUES FOUND

### Issue 1: Wrong Reference Images

The downloaded reference images in `design/references/` have LOW implementation value:

| Image | Rating | Problem |
|-------|--------|---------|
| deck.gl/kepler-gl.jpg | 1/5 | Geospatial map - NOT timeline |
| deck.gl/flowmap.jpg | 1/5 | Flow visualization - NOT Gantt |
| deck.gl/sanddance.jpg | 1/5 | 3D statistical viz - irrelevant |
| linear/screenshots | 1-3/5 | Marketing pages, not UI patterns |
| railnova/railfleet.png | 2/5 | Splash page, not actual software |

The existing `design/screen-*.png` files (AI-generated) serve as inspiration only, not specification.

### Issue 2: Technology Stack Mismatch

Document says "Astro.js 4.x" but project uses Vite + React 18.

**Correct Stack:**
- Vite 5.x (NOT Astro)
- React 18.3.1
- deck.gl 9.2.5
- XState 5.x
- Tailwind CSS

### Issue 3: Sandbox Mode Underspecified

Sandbox Mode is the PRIMARY feature (addresses RFQ pain point #7 "No predictive simulations") but specification is vague. Missing:
- State machine diagram
- Persistence strategy
- Comparison algorithm
- Undo/redo implementation
- Conflict detection rules

### Issue 4: RFQ Pain Points Not Visually Addressed

Many solutions are abstract without concrete specifications.

### Issue 5: File Structure Mismatch

Document assumes Astro structure but project uses `apps/canvas/src/`.

---

## рҹҺҜ NEW REFERENCE SOURCES (Critical - From User)

### Primary References (MUST Study)

| Source | URL | Purpose |
|--------|-----|---------|
| **Epicflow What-If** | https://www.epicflow.com/features/what-if/ | Sandbox Mode UI pattern |
| **Epicflow Pipeline** | https://www.epicflow.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/pipeline-implement.png | Visual planning layout |
| **Dime Scheduler** | https://www.dimescheduler.com/ | EXCELLENT - watch video! |
| **AVS Auto Demo** | https://avs.auto/demo/index.html | Single pane of glass example |
| **DHTMLX Gantt D3** | https://dhtmlx.github.io/demo-gantt-d3/index_1.html | Gantt with deck.gl-like rendering |
| **Prometheus Group** | https://www.prometheusgroup.com/resources/posts/what-does-flipping-burgers-have-to-do-with-managing-asset-or-location-availability | Asset availability patterns |
| **IBM Maximo Scheduler** | Search: "IBM Maximo Maintenance Scheduler" | Industry standard reference |

### Design Philosophy Sources

| Source | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| **Linear App** | Modern aesthetic, dark mode, typography |
| **JetBrains Fleet (Islands UI)** | Enterprise layout density with clarity |
| **Google Maps** | Navigation fluidity (zoom/drag paradigm) |

```

5. https://dhtmlx.github.io/demo-gantt-d3/index_1.html
6. Google Image Search: "IBM Maximo Maintenance Scheduler"
```

1. **Tech Stack**: Vite + React 18 (correct from Astro.js)
2. **Sandbox Mode**: Detailed specification required (will be implemented later)
3. **Scope**: Planning View + Sandbox Mode focused, with roadmap for post-demo features
4. **References**: Use new high-quality sources, screen-*.png as inspiration only
5. **Design Goal**: Modern like Linear, but solid/corporate (not amateur). JetBrains Islands UI is good example.

  "project": "Metro Integrated Planning System (MMS)",

content.content: OlГЎ Claude! Precisamos criar uma versГЈo final da parte visual do app para apresentar nossa proposta ao cliente e ganharmos o processo de licitaГ§ГЈo. O mais importante Г© impressionarmos o cliente visualmente e interativamente com nosso frontend, de modo a demonstrar que podemos solucionar de forma excepcional as dores que ele reportou na RFQ (./docs/RFQ.md). Objetivo: Sua missГЈo Г© criar livremente um documento de UX/Design/Produto que irГЎ descrever a versГЈo final do produto. Este documento Г© para uso interno - nГЈo serГЎ apresentado aos clientes. A prГіxima tarefa serГЎ, atravГ©s deste documento, a implementaГ§ГЈo do frontend interativo com Astro.js e deck.gl, utilizando mocks para os dados. Portanto, o documento deve ser claro e completo com essa finalidade. Formato do documento: - Usar formato .md com o documento escrito em inglГӘs - Incluir um Moodboard com das referГӘncias reais (nГЈo apenas links) - Para cada decisГЈo de tela/soluГ§ГЈo, incluir imagens de exemplo de como deverГЎ ficar, usando screenshots de apps concorrentes ou outras referГӘncias Fontes e ReferГӘncias: - A fonte da verdade Г© exclusivamente a RFQ (docs/RFQ.md) - nГЈo considerar logicamente ou tecnicamente nenhum outro documento ou cГіdigo no projeto - Os demais documentos do projeto podem ser lidos apenas como complemento de pesquisa - acessar as fontes originais e verificar se cada dado se enquadra no novo planejamento - Fazer extensa pesquisa na internet para referГӘncias visuais e soluГ§Гөes ReferГӘncias de Design: - Usar o Linear como referГӘncia principal de design, especialmente a timeline - O "mood" do Linear (espaГ§amentos, cores, tipografia) serve de referГӘncia geral, mas nГЈo as soluГ§Гөes, por se tratar de problemas diferentes - Importante: Diferenciar claramente no documento o que Г© inspirado no Linear vs. o que Г© inspirado em apps de scheduling, manutenГ§ГЈo, ferrovias, etc. - para evitar confusГЈo durante as seГ§Гөes de implementaГ§ГЈo Tela Principal: - A tela mais importante Г© a de planejamento/visГЈo dos dados com a implementaГ§ГЈo do modo "sandbox" que servirГЎ para resolver a solicitaГ§ГЈo de "what-if scenarios" Tecnologia: - EstГЎ decidido e testado que o framework final serГЎ deck.gl - NГЈo implementaremos manualmente features que podem causar problemas cross-browser (zoom, pan, pitch, etc.) - devemos utilizar as melhores opГ§Гөes disponГӯveis para trabalhar com deck.gl Harness: - Utilizar o sistema harness para salvaguardar progresso em caso de necessidade de continuarmos em nova sessГЈo - Registrar o progresso e plano dessa task no harness Utilize modo planejamento e pensamento profundo. Inicie. toolUseResult.stdout: OlГЎ Claude! Precisamos criar uma versГЈo final da parte visual do app para apresentar nossa proposta ao cliente e ganharmos o processo de licitaГ§ГЈo. O mais importante Г© impressionarmos o cliente visualmente e interativamente com nosso frontend, de modo a demonstrar que podemos solucionar de forma excepcional as dores que ele reportou na RFQ (./docs/RFQ.md). Objetivo: Sua missГЈo Г© criar livremente um documento de UX/Design/Produto que irГЎ descrever a versГЈo final do produto. Este documento Г© para uso interno - nГЈo serГЎ apresentado aos clientes. A prГіxima tarefa serГЎ, atravГ©s deste documento, a implementaГ§ГЈo do frontend interativo com Astro.js e deck.gl, utilizando mocks para os dados. Portanto, o documento deve ser claro e completo com essa finalidade. Formato do documento: - Usar formato .md com o documento escrito em inglГӘs - Incluir um Moodboard com das referГӘncias reais (nГЈo apenas links) - Para cada decisГЈo de tela/soluГ§ГЈo, incluir imagens de exemplo de como deverГЎ ficar, usando screenshots de apps concorrentes ou outras referГӘncias Fontes e ReferГӘncias: - A fonte da verdade Г© exclusivamente a RFQ (docs/RFQ.md) - nГЈo considerar logicamente ou tecnicamente nenhum outro documento ou cГіdigo no projeto - Os demais documentos do projeto podem ser lidos apenas como complemento de pesquisa - acessar as fontes originais e verificar se cada dado se enquadra no novo planejamento - Fazer extensa pesquisa na internet para referГӘncias visuais e soluГ§Гөes ReferГӘncias de Design: - Usar o Linear como referГӘncia principal de design, especialmente a timeline - O "mood" do Linear (espaГ§amentos, cores, tipografia) serve de referГӘncia geral, mas nГЈo as soluГ§Гөes, por se tratar de problemas diferentes - Importante: Diferenciar claramente no documento o que Г© inspirado no Linear vs. o que Г© inspirado em apps de scheduling, manutenГ§ГЈo, ferrovias, etc. - para evitar confusГЈo durante as seГ§Гөes de implementaГ§ГЈo Tela Principal: - A tela mais importante Г© a de planejamento/visГЈo dos dados com a implementaГ§ГЈo do modo "sandbox" que servirГЎ para resolver a solicitaГ§ГЈo de "what-if scenarios" Tecnologia: - EstГЎ decidido e testado que o framework final serГЎ deck.gl - NГЈo implementaremos manualmente features que podem causar problemas cross-browser (zoom, pan, pitch, etc.) - devemos utilizar as melhores opГ§Гөes disponГӯveis para trabalhar com deck.gl Harness: - Utilizar o sistema harness para salvaguardar progresso em caso de necessidade de continuarmos em nova sessГЈo - Registrar o progresso e plano dessa task no harness Utilize modo planejamento e pensamento profundo. Inicie. content.content: 1вҶ’# MMS Design Specification 2вҶ’ 3вҶ’> **Internal Document** - Implementation guide for the interactive demo with Astro.js + deck.gl 4вҶ’> 5вҶ’> **Source of Truth**: `docs/RFQ.md` only 6вҶ’> 7вҶ’> **Primary Feature**: Planning View with Sandbox Mode (What-If Scenarios) 8вҶ’ 9вҶ’--- 10вҶ’ 11вҶ’## 1. Executive Summary 12вҶ’ 13вҶ’### 1.1 Objective 14вҶ’ 15вҶ’Win the bidding process by demonstrating an exceptional frontend solution that addresses all client pain points from the RFQ. The demo must visually impress while proving we can deliver a production-ready "Single Pane of Glass" for Rolling Stock Maintenance. 16вҶ’ 17вҶ’### 1.2 RFQ Pain Points вҶ’ Our Solutions 18вҶ’ 19вҶ’| # | Client Pain Point (RFQ) | Our Solution | Key Visual Element | 20вҶ’|---|------------------------|--------------|-------------------| 21вҶ’| 1 | No dedicated mileage management tools | Purpose-built mileage dashboard | KPI Cards + Mileage indicators | 22вҶ’| 2 | IBM Maximo only does asset management | Integrated mileage + maintenance planning | Unified Planning View | 23вҶ’| 3 | Manual Excel management | Interactive visual timeline with drag operations | Gantt-style canvas | 24вҶ’| 4 | Folder-driven data storage | Centralized fleet visualization | Sidebar + Fleet grid | 25вҶ’| 5 | Limited maintenance-operations integration | Single pane for both teams | Shared workspace | 26вҶ’| 6 | Non-compliance risk (underrun/overrun) | Visual alerts + compliance indicators | Color-coded tasks + banners | 27вҶ’| 7 | **No predictive simulations** | **Sandbox Mode for what-if scenarios** | **PRIMARY FEATURE** | 28вҶ’| 8 | Insufficient resource visibility | Resource utilization panels | Heatmap + capacity bars | 29вҶ’| 9 | Manual handling delays | Automated scheduling visualization | Smart conflict detection | 30вҶ’ 31вҶ’--- 32вҶ’ 33вҶ’## 2. Design Philosophy 34вҶ’ 35вҶ’### 2.1 Inspiration Attribution 36вҶ’ 37вҶ’We draw from multiple sources, each contributing specific elements: 38вҶ’ 39вҶ’| Source | What We Take | What We DON'T Take | 40вҶ’|--------|--------------|-------------------| 41вҶ’| **Linear App** | Dark mode aesthetic, typography, whitespace, micro-interactions | Sprint/issue tracking patterns (different domain) | 42вҶ’| **JetBrains Fleet Islands UI** | Modular layout, panel separation, information density | IDE-specific features | 43вҶ’| **DHTMLX Gantt** | Timeline visualization, task bars, dependency lines | Light theme default | 44вҶ’| **Railnova Railfleet** | Fleet dashboard patterns, train status indicators | Specific ECM workflows | 45вҶ’| **deck.gl/Kepler.gl** | GPU-accelerated rendering, smooth transitions | Geospatial focus | 46вҶ’ 47вҶ’### 2.2 Core Principles 48вҶ’ 49вҶ’1. **Density with Clarity** (Islands UI) - Pack information without overwhelming 50вҶ’2. **Dark Mode First** (Linear) - Reduce eye strain, modern aesthetic 51вҶ’3. **Modular Panels** (Fleet) - Visually separated functional areas 52вҶ’4. **Direct Manipulation** (Gantt) - Drag to reschedule, resize to extend 53вҶ’5. **GPU Performance** (deck.gl) - 60fps even with 15k+ tasks 54вҶ’ 55вҶ’--- 56вҶ’ 57вҶ’## 3. # Metro Integrated Planning System - Progress Log

## Current Task: DESIGN-REVISION - Critical Document Revision

### Status: ANALYSIS COMPLETE - Ready for Revision

---

## Session: 2025-12-20 (Harness Analysis & Recovery)

### Task: Analyze context loss and improve harness protocol

**Objective:** Analyze last 2 commits and Claude logs to understand what was done correctly, hallucinations, and context loss.

### Findings

#### 1. Context Loss Analysis

| Metric | Before | After | Lost |
|--------|--------|-------|------|
| claude-progress.txt | 2338 lines | 205 lines | **91%** |
| features.json | 392 lines | 142 lines | **64%** |

**Root Cause:** Sessions saved to harness only at the end, not incrementally.

#### 2. Hallucinations in DESIGN_SPEC.md

| Issue | Expected | Found |
|-------|----------|-------|
| Tech Stack | Vite + React 18 | "Astro.js 4.x" |
| References | Timeline/Gantt examples | Geospatial maps (irrelevant) |
| Sandbox Mode | Detailed state machine | Vague description |

#### 3. What Was Done Correctly

- вң… Created DESIGN_SPEC.md (~1500 lines)
- вң… Identified critical issues in analysis
- вң… Saved plan to `.claude/plans/elegant-sparking-blossom.md`
- вң… Created backup in `docs/archive/`
- вң… Identified new reference sources (Epicflow, Dime Scheduler, AVS)

#### 4. POCs That Exist (from backup)

| POC | Location | Status |
|-----|----------|--------|
| XState Viewport | `stories/01-POCs/State/XStateViewport.stories.tsx` | вң… |
| Semantic Zoom | `stories/01-POCs/Rendering/SemanticZoom.stories.tsx` | вң… |
| Time Scrubber (7 variants) | `stories/01-POCs/TimeScrubber/` | вң… |
| deck.gl Heatmap | `stories/01-POCs/Rendering/` | вң… |
| FractalCanvas | `stories/FractalCanvas*.tsx` | вң… |

### New Harness Rules Added

1. Save BEFORE starting any task
2. Save AFTER each subtask
3. Checkpoint every 15-20 minutes
4. Save before intensive operations
5. If context > 70%, STOP and save

### Anthropic Harness Research (Completed)

Source: [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)

**Key Learnings Applied:**
- JSON preferred for state files (less likely to be overwritten)
- Failure pattern 1: Doing too much вҶ’ context exhaustion
- Failure pattern 2: Declaring done prematurely
- Incremental commits enable recovery
- Session startup rituals: always read context before acting

### Next Steps

1. Continue with DESIGN-REVISION task
2. Fetch new reference screenshots
3. Correct tech stack in DESIGN_SPEC.md

**Status:** Harness protocol improved вң…

---

## Session: 2025-12-19 (Critical Analysis)

### Executive Summary

The DESIGN_SPEC.md document has **CRITICAL issues** that must be resolved before implementation. A thorough analysis was completed and new reference sources were identified by the user.

---

## рҹ”ҙ CRITICAL ISSUES FOUND

### Issue 1: Wrong Reference Images

The downloaded reference images in `design/references/` have LOW implementation value:

| Image | Rating | Problem |
|-------|--------|---------|
| deck.gl/kepler-gl.jpg | 1/5 | Geospatial map - NOT timeline |
| deck.gl/flowmap.jpg | 1/5 | Flow visualization - NOT Gantt |
| deck.gl/sanddance.jpg | 1/5 | 3D statistical viz - irrelevant |
| linear/screenshots | 1-3/5 | Marketing pages, not UI patterns |
| railnova/railfleet.png | 2/5 | Splash page, not actual software |

The existing `design/screen-*.png` files (AI-generated) serve as inspiration only, not specification.

### Issue 2: Technology Stack Mismatch

Document says "Astro.js 4.x" but project uses Vite + React 18.

**Correct Stack:**
- Vite 5.x (NOT Astro)
- React 18.3.1
- deck.gl 9.2.5
- XState 5.x
- Tailwind CSS

### Issue 3: Sandbox Mode Underspecified

Sandbox Mode is the PRIMARY feature (addresses RFQ pain point #7 "No predictive simulations") but specification is vague. Missing:
- State machine diagram
- Persistence strategy
- Comparison algorithm
- Undo/redo implementation
- Conflict detection rules

### Issue 4: RFQ Pain Points Not Visually Addressed

Many solutions are abstract without concrete specifications.

### Issue 5: File Structure Mismatch

Document assumes Astro structure but project uses `apps/canvas/src/`.

---

## рҹҺҜ NEW REFERENCE SOURCES (Critical - From User)

### Primary References (MUST Study)

| Source | URL | Purpose |
|--------|-----|---------|
| **Epicflow What-If** | https://www.epicflow.com/features/what-if/ | Sandbox Mode UI pattern |
| **Epicflow Pipeline** | https://www.epicflow.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/pipeline-implement.png | Visual planning layout |
| **Dime Scheduler** | https://www.dimescheduler.com/ | EXCELLENT - watch video! |
| **AVS Auto Demo** | https://avs.auto/demo/index.html | Single pane of glass example |
| **DHTMLX Gantt D3** | https://dhtmlx.github.io/demo-gantt-d3/index_1.html | Gantt with deck.gl-like rendering |
| **Prometheus Group** | https://www.prometheusgroup.com/resources/posts/what-does-flipping-burgers-have-to-do-with-managing-asset-or-location-availability | Asset availability patterns |
| **IBM Maximo Scheduler** | Search: "IBM Maximo Maintenance Scheduler" | Industry standard reference |

### Design Philosophy Sources

| Source | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| **Linear App** | Modern aesthetic, dark mode, typography |
| **JetBrains Fleet (Islands UI)** | Enterprise layout density with clarity |
| **Google Maps** | Navigation fluidity (zoom/drag paradigm) |

### Screenshots to Capture (Next Session)

```
1. https://www.epicflow.com/features/what-if/ (what-if toggle, delta panel)
2. https://www.epicflow.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/pipeline-implement.png
3. https://www.dimescheduler.com/ (video + screenshots)
4. https://avs.auto/demo/index.html (single pane example)
5. https://dhtmlx.github.io/demo-gantt-d3/index_1.html
6. Google Image Search: "IBM Maximo Maintenance Scheduler"
```

---

## User Decisions (Confirmed 2025-12-19)

1. **Tech Stack**: Vite + React 18 (correct from Astro.js)
2. **Sandbox Mode**: Detailed specification required (will be implemented later)
3. **Scope**: Planning View + Sandbox Mode focused, with roadmap for post-demo features
4. **References**: Use new high-quality sources, screen-*.png as inspiration only
5. **Design Goal**: Modern like Linear, but solid/corporate (not amateur). JetBrains Islands UI is good example.

---

## Revised Document Structure

### Part 1: Vision & RFQ Mapping
- Pain points вҶ’ visual solutions вҶ’ screen reference
- Demo focus: Planning View + Sandbox Mode
- Post-demo roadmap

### Part 2: Moodboard (REWRITE NEEDED)
**Primary Sources:**
- Epicflow (what-if, pipeline)
- Dime Scheduler (enterprise scheduling)
- AVS Demo (single pane)
- DHTMLX Gantt D3 (rendering)

**Secondary Sources:**
- Linear (aesthetic)
- JetBrains Fleet (Islands layout)
- screen-*.png (internal inspiration)

**Remove/Demote:**
- deck.gl geospatial images (irrelevant)
- Railnova splash page
- Linear marketing pages

### Part 3: Design System (Keep)
### Part 4: Application Shell (Update tech stack)
### Part 5: Planning View (Enhance with LOD references)
### Part 6: Sandbox Mode (MAJOR REWRITE with state machine)
### Part 7: Components (Keep)
### Part 8: Data Structures (Enhance)
### Part 9: Implementation (REWRITE tech stack)
### Part 10: Roadmap (NEW section)

---

## Demo Focus

### MUST Work Perfectly
1. Semantic Zoom (MACROвҶ’MESOвҶ’MICRO)
2. Sandbox Mode Toggle (violet accent)
3. Delta Metrics (impact visualization)
4. Visual polish (modern, not amateur)

### Can Be Mock/Fake
1. Mileage calculations (hardcoded)
2. Reports
3. Notifications
4. Maximo integration

---

## Next Steps (Next Session)

1. **Fetch new reference screenshots** from Epicflow, Dime Scheduler, AVS, DHTMLX, IBM Maximo
2. **Rewrite Moodboard section** with new sources
3. **Correct tech stack** throughout document (Astro вҶ’ Vite + React 18)
4. **Add detailed Sandbox Mode specification**:
   - XState state machine diagram
   - localStorage persistence schema
   - Comparison algorithm
   - Undo/redo stack
   - Conflict detection rules
5. **Update file structure** to match reality
6. **Add roadmap section** for post-demo features
7. **Simplify Storybook phases** for demo focus

---

# MMS Design Specification > **Internal Document** - Implementation guide for the interactive demo with Astro.js + deck.gl > > **Source of Truth**: `docs/RFQ.md` only > > **Primary Feature**: Planning View with Sandbox Mode (What-If Scenarios) --- ## 1. Executive Summary ### 1.1 Objective Win the bidding process by demonstrating an exceptional frontend solution that addresses all client pain points from the RFQ. The demo must visually impress while proving we can deliver a production-ready "Single Pane of Glass" for Rolling Stock Maintenance. ### 1.2 RFQ Pain Points вҶ’ Our Solutions | # | Client Pain Point (RFQ) | Our Solution | Key Visual Element | |---|------------------------|--------------|-------------------| | 1 | No dedicated mileage management tools | Purpose-built mileage dashboard | KPI Cards + Mileage indicators | | 2 | IBM Maximo only does asset management | Integrated mileage + maintenance planning | Unified Planning View | | 3 | Manual Excel management | Interactive visual timeline with drag operations | Gantt-style canvas | | 4 | Folder-driven data storage | Centralized fleet visualization | Sidebar + Fleet grid | | 5 | Limited maintenance-operations integration | Single pane for both teams | Shared workspace | | 6 | Non-compliance risk (underrun/overrun) | Visual alerts + compliance indicators | Color-coded tasks + banners | | 7 | **No predictive simulations** | **Sandbox Mode for what-if scenarios** | **PRIMARY FEATURE** | | 8 | Insufficient resource visibility | Resource utilization panels | Heatmap + capacity bars | | 9 | Manual handling delays | Automated scheduling visualization | Smart conflict detection | --- ## 2. Design Philosophy ### 2.1 Inspiration Attribution We draw from multiple sources, each contributing specific elements: | Source | What We Take | What We DON'T Take | |--------|--------------|-------------------| | **Linear App** | Dark mode aesthetic, typography, whitespace, micro-interactions | Sprint/issue tracking patterns (different domain) | | **JetBrains Fleet Islands UI** | Modular layout, panel separation, information density | IDE-specific features | | **DHTMLX Gantt** | Timeline visualization, task bars, dependency lines | Light theme default | | **Railnova Railfleet** | Fleet dashboard patterns, train status indicators | Specific ECM workflows | | **deck.gl/Kepler.gl** | GPU-accelerated rendering, smooth transitions | Geospatial focus | ### 2.2 Core Principles 1. **Density with Clarity** (Islands UI) - Pack information without overwhelming 2. **Dark Mode First** (Linear) - Reduce eye strain, modern aesthetic 3. **Modular Panels** (Fleet) - Visually separated functional areas 4. **Direct Manipulation** (Gantt) - Drag to reschedule, resize to extend 5. **GPU Performance** (deck.gl) - 60fps even with 15k+ tasks --- ## 3. Moodboard ### 3.1 Aesthetic Reference: Linear App The visual "mood" comes from Linear - minimalist, dark, typography-focused. *Linear: Dark mode default, generous whitespace, bold typography* *Linear: Multi-layered views with clean tab navigation* ### 3.2 Layout Reference: JetBrains Fleet Islands UI Complex information organized into distinct "islands" - perfect for enterprise apps. *Fleet Islands UI: Modular panels, transparent styling, reduced cognitive load* *GitKraken: Similar density with clarity approach* ### 3.3 Domain Reference: Scheduling Software Gantt chart patterns from scheduling software. *DHTMLX Gantt: Dark theme, task bars with dependencies, timeline header* ### 3.4 Domain Reference: Railway Software Fleet management patterns from railway industry. *Railfleet: ECM fleet management, maintenance workflows* ### 3.5 Rendering Reference: deck.gl GPU-accelerated visualization capabilities. *Kepler.gl: deck.gl-powered data visualization* *Flowmap.blue: Timeline-based flow visualization* *SandDance: Interactive data exploration with smooth transitions* --- ## 4. Design System ### 4.1 Color Palette (Dark Mode) ``` BACKGROUND (Islands UI inspired) в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ bg-canvas: #0A0A0A (Main canvas background) bg-panel: #141414 (Island panels) bg-elevated: #1C1C1C (Cards, popovers) bg-hover: #262626 (Interactive hover) bg-active: #2A2A2A (Active/selected) BORDERS (Subtle separation) в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ border-subtle: #1F1F1F (Panel edges) border-default: #2E2E2E (Dividers) border-strong: #404040 (Focus rings) TEXT (High contrast) в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ text-primary: #FAFAFA (Headlines) text-secondary: #A1A1A1 (Body) text-tertiary: #6B6B6B (Labels) text-disabled: #404040 (Disabled) SEMANTIC - Data Structures ### 9.1 Fleet Data (From RFQ) ```typescript // Fleet composition from RFQ const FLEET_CONFIG = { ALSTOM: { count: 79, prefix: 'ALT', depot: 'AL_RASHIDIYA' }, KINKI: { count: 49, prefix: 'KNK', depot: 'JEBEL_ALI' }, total: 128, }; // Contract period from RFQ const CONTRACT = { startYear: 2024, endYear: 2036, // 12 years }; // Maintenance intervals from RFQ const MAINTENANCE_TYPES = { 'A-Check': { intervalKm: 10_000, tolerance: 2_000, days: 1, manHours: 8 }, 'B-Check': { intervalKm: 50_000, tolerance: 5_000, days: 2, manHours: 32 }, 'C-Check': { intervalKm: 200_000, tolerance: 20_000, days: 3, manHours: 160 }, 'D-Overhaul': { intervalKm: 800_000, tolerance: 50_000, days: 20, manHours: 1200 }, }; ``` ### 9.2 Train Entity ```typescript interface Train { id: string; // "ALT-001" or "KNK-001" type: 'ALSTOM' | 'KINKI'; name: string; depot: 'AL_RASHIDIYA' | 'JEBEL_ALI'; currentMileageKm: number; avgDailyMileageKm: number; status: 'operational' | 'maintenance' | 'out-of-service'; lastMaintenanceDate: string; // ISO 8601 nextMaintenanceDue: string; // ISO 8601 } ``` ### 9.3 Task Entity ```typescript interface Task { id: string; trainId: string; startDate: string; // ISO 8601 endDate: string; // ISO 8601 type: 'A-Check' | 'B-Check' | 'C-Check' | 'D-Overhaul'; status: 'scheduled' | 'in-progress' | 'completed' | 'cancelled'; manHours: number; track: number; // 1-5 hasConflict: boolean; conflictReason?: string; dependencies?: string[]; // Task IDs estimatedCost: number; } ``` ### 9.4 Scenario Entity (Sandbox) ```typescript interface Scenario { id: string; name: string; description?: string; createdAt: string; modifiedAt: string; changes: ScenarioChange[]; metrics: { opexDelta: number; // vs baseline overrunRiskCount: number; trackUtilizationDelta: number; }; } interface ScenarioChange { type: 'move' | 'resize' | 'create' | 'delete' | 'modify'; taskId: string; before: Partial | null; after: Partial | null; timestamp: string; } ``` --- ## 10. Interactions & Gestures ### 10.1 Canvas Navigation | Input | Action | Handler | |-------|--------|---------| | Drag | Pan canvas | @use-gesture onDrag | | Scroll wheel | Zoom (cursor-centered) | @use-gesture onWheel | | Pinch (touch) | Zoom | @use-gesture onPinch | | Double-click | Zoom in to point | Custom handler | | Double-tap (touch) | Zoom in to point | Custom handler | ### 10.2 Task Interactions | Input | Live Mode | Sandbox Mode | |-------|-----------|--------------| | Hover | Tooltip | Tooltip + resize handles | | Click | Select task | Select task | | Drag task | - | Move (reschedule) | | Drag edge | - | Resize duration | | Right-click | View menu | Edit menu | | Delete key | - | Delete selected | ### 10.3 Keyboard Shortcuts | Key | Action | |-----|--------| | `+` / `=` | Zoom in | | `-` | Zoom out | | `0` | Reset zoom to 1x | | Arrow keys | Pan canvas | | `Escape` | Clear selection | | `Delete` | Delete task (Sandbox) | | `Ctrl+Z` | Undo (Sandbox) | | `Ctrl+Shift+Z` | Redo (Sandbox) | | `S` | Toggle Sandbox mode | --- ## 11. navigation works вң“ Ready for client presentation ``` ### 15.6 Storybook Configuration **Recommended Storybook Setup:** ```ts // .storybook/main.ts export default { stories: [ .storybook/preview.ts export const parameters = { backgrounds: { default: 'dark', values: [ { name: 'dark', value: '#0A0A0A' }, { name: 'sandbox', value: '#0F0A1A' }, ], }, viewport: { viewports: { Organization:** ``` stories/ в”ңв”Җв”Җ 01-wireframes/ # Phase 1: Low-fidelity в”ңв”Җв”Җ 02-layout/ # Phase 2: Design system applied в”ңв”Җв”Җ 03-components/ # Phase 3: Interactive components в”Ӯ 04-pages/ # Phase 4: Full compositions в”ңв”Җв”Җ 05-animations/ # Phase 4: Motion design в””в”Җв”Җ 06-edge-cases/ # Phase 4: Error states, stress tests ``` ### 15.7 Quality Gates Each phase must pass quality gates before proceeding: | Phase | Gate | Criteria | |-------|------|----------| | 1 | **Layout Approval** | Stakeholder sign-off on information architecture | | 2 | **Design Approval** | Visual design meets enterprise standards | | 3 | **Functionality Approval** | All interactions work correctly | | 4 | **Release Approval** | Performance, accessibility, polish complete | **Enterprise Quality Checklist:** ``` VISUAL QUALITY (Not "Amateur") в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ в–Ў Consistent spacing (8px grid strictly followed) в–Ў No orphaned elements (everything aligned) в–Ў Typography hierarchy clear at a glance в–Ў Colors have sufficient contrast (WCAG AA) в–Ў No default browser styles visible в–Ў Loading states prevent layout shift в–Ў Error states are informative, not scary в–Ў Empty states guide the user INTERACTION QUALITY в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ в–Ў Hover states on all interactive elements в–Ў Focus indicators visible (keyboard nav) в–Ў Click targets minimum 44Г—44px в–Ў Tooltips appear on hover (300ms delay) в–Ў Drag operations have visual feedback в–Ў Undo is always available in Sandbox PERFORMANCE QUALITY в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ в–Ў Initial load < 3s в–Ў 60fps during pan/zoom в–Ў No jank during LOD transitions в–Ў Viewport culling active (only visible rendered) в–Ў Memory stable over 5min session POLISH в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ в–Ў Micro-animations add delight, not distraction в–Ў Transitions are 200-300ms, easing applied в–Ў Cursors indicate interaction affordance в–Ў Favicon and page title set в–Ў Console has no warnings/errors ``` --- *Document Version: 2.1* *Created: 2025-12-19* *Last Updated: 2025-12-19* *Author: Claude Code (AI-assisted)*

# MMS Design Specification > **Internal Document** - Implementation guide for the interactive demo with Vite + React 18 + deck.gl > > **Source of Truth**: `docs/RFQ.md` only > > **Primary Feature**: Planning View with Sandbox Mode (What-If Scenarios) --- ## 1. Executive Summary ### 1.1 Objective Win the bidding process by demonstrating an exceptional frontend solution that addresses all client pain points from the RFQ. The demo must visually impress while proving we can deliver a production-ready "Single Pane of Glass" for Rolling Stock Maintenance. ### 1.2 RFQ Pain Points вҶ’ Our Solutions | # | Client Pain Point (RFQ) | Our Solution | Key Visual Element | |---|------------------------|--------------|-------------------| | 1 | No dedicated mileage management tools | Purpose-built mileage dashboard | KPI Cards + Mileage indicators | | 2 | IBM Maximo only does asset management | Integrated mileage + maintenance planning | Unified Planning View | | 3 | Manual Excel management | Interactive visual timeline with drag operations | Gantt-style canvas | | 4 | Folder-driven data storage | Centralized fleet visualization | Sidebar + Fleet grid | | 5 | Limited maintenance-operations integration | Single pane for both teams | Shared workspace | | 6 | Non-compliance risk (underrun/overrun) | Visual alerts + compliance indicators | Color-coded tasks + banners | | 7 | **No predictive simulations** | **Sandbox Mode for what-if scenarios** | **PRIMARY FEATURE** | | 8 | Insufficient resource visibility | Resource utilization panels | Heatmap + capacity bars | | 9 | Manual handling delays | Automated scheduling visualization | Smart conflict detection | --- ## 2. Design Philosophy ### 2.1 Inspiration Attribution We draw from multiple sources, each contributing specific elements: | Source | What We Take | What We DON'T Take | |--------|--------------|-------------------| | **Linear App** | Dark mode aesthetic, typography, whitespace, micro-interactions | Sprint/issue tracking patterns (different domain) | | **JetBrains Fleet Islands UI** | Modular layout, panel separation, information density | IDE-specific features | | **DHTMLX Gantt** | Timeline visualization, task bars, dependency lines | Light theme default | | **Railnova Railfleet** | Fleet dashboard patterns, train status indicators | Specific ECM workflows | | **deck.gl/Kepler.gl** | GPU-accelerated rendering, smooth transitions | Geospatial focus | ### 2.2 Core Principles 1. **Density with Clarity** (Islands UI) - Pack information without overwhelming 2. **Dark Mode First** (Linear) - Reduce eye strain, modern aesthetic 3. **Modular Panels** (Fleet) - Visually separated functional areas 4. **Direct Manipulation** (Gantt) - Drag to reschedule, resize to extend 5. **GPU Performance** (deck.gl) - 60fps even with 15k+ tasks --- ## 3. Moodboard ### 3.1 Aesthetic Reference: Linear App The visual "mood" comes from Linear - minimalist, dark, typography-focused. *Linear: Dark mode default, generous whitespace, bold typography* *Linear: Multi-layered views with clean tab navigation* ### 3.2 Layout Reference: JetBrains Fleet Islands UI Complex information organized into distinct "islands" - perfect for enterprise apps. *Fleet Islands UI: Modular panels, transparent styling, reduced cognitive load* *GitKraken: Similar density with clarity approach* ### 3.3 Domain Reference: Scheduling Software Gantt chart patterns from scheduling software. *DHTMLX Gantt: Dark theme, task bars with dependencies, timeline header* ### 3.4 Domain Reference: Railway Software Fleet management patterns from railway industry. *Railfleet: ECM fleet management, maintenance workflows* ### 3.5 Rendering Reference: deck.gl GPU-accelerated visualization capabilities. *Kepler.gl: deck.gl-powered data visualization* *Flowmap.blue: Timeline-based flow visualization* *SandDance: Interactive data exploration with smooth transitions* --- ## 4. Design System ### 4.1 Color Palette (Dark Mode) ``` BACKGROUND (Islands UI inspired) в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ bg-canvas: #0A0A0A (Main canvas background) bg-panel: #141414 (Island panels) bg-elevated: #1C1C1C (Cards, popovers) bg-hover: #262626 (Interactive hover) bg-active: #2A2A2A (Active/selected) BORDERS (Subtle separation) в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ border-subtle: #1F1F1F (Panel edges) border-default: #2E2E2E (Dividers) border-strong: #404040 (Focus rings) TEXT (High contrast) в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ text-primary: #FAFAFA (Headlines) text-secondary: #A1A1A1 (Body) text-tertiary: #6B6B6B (Labels) text-disabled: #404040 (Disabled) SEMANTIC - MAINTENANCE TYPES (From RFQ) в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ a-check: #10B981 (Green - Quick, 10k km) b-check: #3B82F6 (Blue - Data Structures ### 9.1 Fleet Data (From RFQ) ```typescript // Fleet composition from RFQ const FLEET_CONFIG = { ALSTOM: { count: 79, prefix: 'ALT', depot: 'AL_RASHIDIYA' }, KINKI: { count: 49, prefix: 'KNK', depot: 'JEBEL_ALI' }, total: 128, }; // Contract period from RFQ const CONTRACT = { startYear: 2024, endYear: 2036, // 12 years }; // Maintenance intervals from RFQ const MAINTENANCE_TYPES = { 'A-Check': { intervalKm: 10_000, tolerance: 2_000, days: 1, manHours: 8 }, 'B-Check': { intervalKm: 50_000, tolerance: 5_000, days: 2, manHours: 32 }, 'C-Check': { intervalKm: 200_000, tolerance: 20_000, days: 3, manHours: 160 }, 'D-Overhaul': { intervalKm: 800_000, tolerance: 50_000, days: 20, manHours: 1200 }, }; ``` ### 9.2 Train Entity ```typescript interface Train { id: string; // "ALT-001" or "KNK-001" type: 'ALSTOM' | 'KINKI'; name: string; depot: 'AL_RASHIDIYA' | 'JEBEL_ALI'; currentMileageKm: number; avgDailyMileageKm: number; status: 'operational' | 'maintenance' | 'out-of-service'; lastMaintenanceDate: string; // ISO 8601 nextMaintenanceDue: string; // ISO 8601 } ``` ### 9.3 Task Entity ```typescript interface Task { id: string; trainId: string; startDate: string; // ISO 8601 endDate: string; // ISO 8601 type: 'A-Check' | 'B-Check' | 'C-Check' | 'D-Overhaul'; status: 'scheduled' | 'in-progress' | 'completed' | 'cancelled'; manHours: number; track: number; // 1-5 hasConflict: boolean; conflictReason?: string; dependencies?: string[]; // Task IDs estimatedCost: number; } ``` ### 9.4 Scenario Entity (Sandbox) ```typescript interface Scenario { id: string; name: string; description?: string; createdAt: string; modifiedAt: string; changes: ScenarioChange[]; metrics: { opexDelta: number; // vs baseline overrunRiskCount: number; trackUtilizationDelta: number; }; } interface ScenarioChange { type: 'move' | 'resize' | 'create' | 'delete' | 'modify'; taskId: string; before: Partial | null; after: Partial | null; timestamp: string; } ``` --- ## 10. Interactions & Gestures ### 10.1 Canvas Navigation | Input | Action | Handler | |-------|--------|---------| | Drag | Pan canvas | @use-gesture onDrag | | Scroll wheel | Zoom (cursor-centered) | @use-gesture onWheel | | Pinch (touch) | Zoom | @use-gesture onPinch | | Double-click | Zoom in to point | Custom handler | | Double-tap (touch) | Zoom in to point | Custom handler | ### 10.2 Task Interactions | Input | Live Mode | Sandbox Mode | |-------|-----------|--------------| | Hover | Tooltip | Tooltip + resize handles | | Click | Select task | Select task | | Drag task | - | Move (reschedule) | | Drag edge | - | Resize duration | | Right-click | View menu | Edit menu | | Delete key | - | Delete selected | ### 10.3 Keyboard Shortcuts | Key | Action | |-----|--------| | `+` / `=` | Zoom in | | `-` | Zoom out | | `0` | Reset zoom to 1x | | Arrow keys | Pan canvas | | `Escape` | Clear selection | | `Delete` | Delete task (Sandbox) | | `Ctrl+Z` | Undo (Sandbox) | | `Ctrl+Shift+Z` | Redo (Sandbox) | | `S` | Toggle Sandbox mode | --- ## 11. toolUseResult.originalFile:
# MMS Design Specification > **Internal Document** - Implementation guide for the interactive demo with Vite + React 18 + deck.gl > > **Source of Truth**: `docs/RFQ.md` only > > **Primary Feature**: Planning View with Sandbox Mode (What-If Scenarios) --- ## 1. Executive Summary ### 1.1 Objective Win the bidding process by demonstrating an exceptional frontend solution that addresses all client pain points from the RFQ. The demo must visually impress while proving we can deliver a production-ready "Single Pane of Glass" for Rolling Stock Maintenance. ### 1.2 RFQ Pain Points вҶ’ Our Solutions | # | Client Pain Point (RFQ) | Our Solution | Key Visual Element | |---|------------------------|--------------|-------------------| | 1 | No dedicated mileage management tools | Purpose-built mileage dashboard | KPI Cards + Mileage indicators | | 2 | IBM Maximo only does asset management | Integrated mileage + maintenance planning | Unified Planning View | | 3 | Manual Excel management | Interactive visual timeline with drag operations | Gantt-style canvas | | 4 | Folder-driven data storage | Centralized fleet visualization | Sidebar + Fleet grid | | 5 | Limited maintenance-operations integration | Single pane for both teams | Shared workspace | | 6 | Non-compliance risk (underrun/overrun) | Visual alerts + compliance indicators | Color-coded tasks + banners | | 7 | **No predictive simulations** | **Sandbox Mode for what-if scenarios** | **PRIMARY FEATURE** | | 8 | Insufficient resource visibility | Resource utilization panels | Heatmap + capacity bars | | 9 | Manual handling delays | Automated scheduling visualization | Smart conflict detection | --- ## 2. Design Philosophy ### 2.1 Inspiration Attribution We draw from multiple sources, each contributing specific elements: | Source | What We Take | What We DON'T Take | |--------|--------------|-------------------| | **Linear App** | Dark mode aesthetic, typography, whitespace, micro-interactions | Sprint/issue tracking patterns (different domain) | | **JetBrains Fleet Islands UI** | Modular layout, panel separation, information density | IDE-specific features | | **DHTMLX Gantt** | Timeline visualization, task bars, dependency lines | Light theme default | | **Railnova Railfleet** | Fleet dashboard patterns, train status indicators | Specific ECM workflows | | **deck.gl/Kepler.gl** | GPU-accelerated rendering, smooth transitions | Geospatial focus | ### 2.2 Core Principles 1. **Density with Clarity** (Islands UI) - Pack information without overwhelming 2. **Dark Mode First** (Linear) - Reduce eye strain, modern aesthetic 3. **Modular Panels** (Fleet) - Visually separated functional areas 4. **Direct Manipulation** (Gantt) - Drag to reschedule, resize to extend 5. **GPU Performance** (deck.gl) - 60fps even with 15k+ tasks --- ## 3. Moodboard ### 3.1 Aesthetic Reference: Linear App The visual "mood" comes from Linear - minimalist, dark, typography-focused. *Linear: Dark mode default, generous whitespace, bold typography* *Linear: Multi-layered views with clean tab navigation* ### 3.2 Layout Reference: JetBrains Fleet Islands UI Complex information organized into distinct "islands" - perfect for enterprise apps. *Fleet Islands UI: Modular panels, transparent styling, reduced cognitive load* *GitKraken: Similar density with clarity approach* ### 3.3 Domain Reference: Scheduling Software Gantt chart patterns from scheduling software. *DHTMLX Gantt: Dark theme, task bars with dependencies, timeline header* ### 3.4 Domain Reference: Railway Software Fleet management patterns from railway industry. *Railfleet: ECM fleet management, maintenance workflows* ### 3.5 Rendering Reference: deck.gl GPU-accelerated visualization capabilities. *Kepler.gl: deck.gl-powered data visualization* *Flowmap.blue: Timeline-based flow visualization* *SandDance: Interactive data exploration with smooth transitions* --- ## 4. Design System ### 4.1 Color Palette (Dark Mode) ``` BACKGROUND (Islands UI inspired) в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ bg-canvas: #0A0A0A (Main canvas background) bg-panel: #141414 (Island panels) bg-elevated: #1C1C1C (Cards, popovers) bg-hover: #262626 (Interactive hover) bg-active: #2A2A2A (Active/selected) BORDERS (Subtle separation) в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ border-subtle: #1F1F1F (Panel edges) border-default: #2E2E2E (Dividers) border-strong: #404040 (Focus rings) TEXT (High contrast) в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ text-primary: #FAFAFA (Headlines) text-secondary: #A1A1A1 (Body) text-tertiary: #6B6B6B (Labels) text-disabled: #404040 (Disabled) SEMANTIC - MAINTENANCE TYPES (From RFQ) в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ a-check: #10B981 (Green - Quick, 10k km) b-check: #3B82F6 (Blue - Standard, 50k km) c-check: #F59E0B (Amber - Major, 200k km) d-overhaul: #EF4444 (Red - Full, 800k km) SEMANTIC - STATUS в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ success: #10B981 (Compliant) warning: #F59E0B (Approaching limit) danger: #EF4444 (Overrun/conflict) info: #3B82F6 (Informational) HEATMAP GRADIENT (Capacity/Workload) в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ low: #FEF3C7 (Yellow-100) medium: #FB923C (Orange-400) high: #DC2626 (Red-600) critical: #7F1D1D (Red-900) SANDBOX MODE ACCENT (Distinct from baseline) в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ sandbox-accent: #8B5CF6 (Violet-500) sandbox-bg: #0F0A1A (Violet-tinted canvas) sandbox-border: #6D28D9 (Violet-700) ``` ### 4.2 Typography ``` FONT FAMILY (Fleet-inspired) в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ Primary: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif Monospace: JetBrains Mono, monospace (IDs, numbers) SCALE в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ h1: 24px / 600 / -0.02em (Page titles) h2: 18px / 600 / -0.01em (Section headers) h3: 15px / 600 / 0 (Panel titles) body: 13px / 400 / 0 (Default text) caption: 11px / 500 / 0.02em (Labels) mono: 12px / 500 / 0 (Train IDs, dates) ``` ### 4.3 Spacing & Radius ``` SPACING (4px base unit) в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ xs: 4px (Tight gaps) sm: 8px (Default gap) md: 12px (Panel padding) lg: 16px (Section gaps) xl: 24px (Major sections) 2xl: 32px (Page margins) RADIUS (Islands UI - subtle rounding) в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ sm: 4px (Buttons, inputs) md: 6px (Cards, panels) lg: 8px (Modals) full: 9999px (Pills, badges) ``` --- ## 5. Data Structures ### 9.1 Fleet Data (From RFQ) ```typescript // Fleet composition from RFQ const FLEET_CONFIG = { ALSTOM: { count: 79, prefix: 'ALT', depot: 'AL_RASHIDIYA' }, KINKI: { count: 49, prefix: 'KNK', depot: 'JEBEL_ALI' }, total: 128, }; // Contract period from RFQ const CONTRACT = { startYear: 2024, endYear: 2036, // 12 years }; // Maintenance intervals from RFQ const MAINTENANCE_TYPES = { 'A-Check': { intervalKm: 10_000, tolerance: 2_000, days: 1, manHours: 8 }, 'B-Check': { intervalKm: 50_000, tolerance: 5_000, days: 2, manHours: 32 }, 'C-Check': { intervalKm: 200_000, tolerance: 20_000, days: 3, manHours: 160 }, 'D-Overhaul': { intervalKm: 800_000, tolerance: 50_000, days: 20, manHours: 1200 }, }; ``` ### 9.2 Train Entity ```typescript interface Train { id: string; // "ALT-001" or "KNK-001" type: 'ALSTOM' | 'KINKI'; name: string; depot: 'AL_RASHIDIYA' | 'JEBEL_ALI'; currentMileageKm: number; avgDailyMileageKm: number; status: 'operational' | 'maintenance' | 'out-of-service'; lastMaintenanceDate: string; // ISO 8601 nextMaintenanceDue: string; // ISO 8601 } ``` ### 9.3 Task Entity ```typescript interface Task { id: string; trainId: string; startDate: string; // ISO 8601 endDate: string; // ISO 8601 type: 'A-Check' | 'B-Check' | 'C-Check' | 'D-Overhaul'; status: 'scheduled' | 'in-progress' | 'completed' | 'cancelled'; manHours: number; track: number; // 1-5 hasConflict: boolean; conflictReason?: string; dependencies?: string[]; // Task IDs estimatedCost: number; } ``` ### 9.4 Scenario Entity (Sandbox) ```typescript interface Scenario { id: string; name: string; description?: string; createdAt: string; modifiedAt: string; changes: ScenarioChange[]; metrics: { opexDelta: number; // vs baseline overrunRiskCount: number; trackUtilizationDelta: number; }; } interface ScenarioChange { type: 'move' | 'resize' | 'create' | 'delete' | 'modify'; taskId: string; before: Partial | null; after: Partial | null; timestamp: string; } ``` --- ## 10. Interactions & Gestures ### 10.1 Canvas Navigation | Input | Action | Handler | |-------|--------|---------| | Drag | Pan canvas | @use-gesture onDrag | | Scroll wheel | Zoom (cursor-centered) | @use-gesture onWheel | | Pinch (touch) | Zoom | @use-gesture onPinch | | Double-click | Zoom in to point | Custom handler | | Double-tap (touch) | Zoom in to point | Custom handler | ### 10.2 Task Interactions | Input | Live Mode | Sandbox Mode | |-------|-----------|--------------| | Hover | Tooltip | Tooltip + resize handles | | Click | Select task | Select task | | Drag task | - | Move (reschedule) | | Drag edge | - | Resize duration | | Right-click | View menu | Edit menu | | Delete key | - | Delete selected | ### 10.3 Keyboard Shortcuts | Key | Action | |-----|--------| | `+` / `=` | Zoom in | | `-` | Zoom out | | `0` | Reset zoom to 1x | | Arrow keys | Pan canvas | | `Escape` | Clear selection | | `Delete` | Delete task (Sandbox) | | `Ctrl+Z` | Undo (Sandbox) | | `Ctrl+Shift+Z` | Redo (Sandbox) | | `S` | Toggle Sandbox mode | --- ## 11. content.content: OlГЎ Claude! Precisamos criar uma versГЈo final da parte visual do app para apresentar nossa proposta ao cliente e ganharmos o processo de licitaГ§ГЈo. O mais importante Г© impressionarmos o cliente visualmente e interativamente com nosso frontend, de modo a demonstrar que podemos solucionar de forma excepcional as dores que ele reportou na RFQ (./docs/RFQ.md). Objetivo: Sua missГЈo Г© criar livremente um documento de UX/Design/Produto que irГЎ descrever a versГЈo final do produto. Este documento Г© para uso interno - nГЈo serГЎ apresentado aos clientes. A prГіxima tarefa serГЎ, atravГ©s deste documento, a implementaГ§ГЈo do frontend interativo com Astro.js e deck.gl, utilizando mocks para os dados. Portanto, o documento deve ser claro e completo com essa finalidade. Formato do documento: - Usar formato .md com o documento escrito em inglГӘs - Incluir um Moodboard com das referГӘncias reais (nГЈo apenas links) - Para cada decisГЈo de tela/soluГ§ГЈo, incluir imagens de exemplo de como deverГЎ ficar, usando screenshots de apps concorrentes ou outras referГӘncias Fontes e ReferГӘncias: - A fonte da verdade Г© exclusivamente a RFQ (docs/RFQ.md) - nГЈo considerar logicamente ou tecnicamente nenhum outro documento ou cГіdigo no projeto - Os demais documentos do projeto podem ser lidos apenas como complemento de pesquisa - acessar as fontes originais e verificar se cada dado se enquadra no novo planejamento - Fazer extensa pesquisa na internet para referГӘncias visuais e soluГ§Гөes ReferГӘncias de Design: - Usar o Linear como referГӘncia principal de design, especialmente a timeline - O "mood" do Linear (espaГ§amentos, cores, tipografia) serve de referГӘncia geral, mas nГЈo as soluГ§Гөes, por se tratar de problemas diferentes - Importante: Diferenciar claramente no documento o que Г© inspirado no Linear vs. o que Г© inspirado em apps de scheduling, manutenГ§ГЈo, ferrovias, etc. - para evitar confusГЈo durante as seГ§Гөes de implementaГ§ГЈo Tela Principal: - A tela mais importante Г© a de planejamento/visГЈo dos dados com a implementaГ§ГЈo do modo "sandbox" que servirГЎ para resolver a solicitaГ§ГЈo de "what-if scenarios" Tecnologia: - EstГЎ decidido e testado que o framework final serГЎ deck.gl - NГЈo implementaremos manualmente features que podem causar problemas cross-browser (zoom, pan, pitch, etc.) - devemos utilizar as melhores opГ§Гөes disponГӯveis para trabalhar com deck.gl Harness: - Utilizar o sistema harness para salvaguardar progresso em caso de necessidade de continuarmos em nova sessГЈo - Registrar o progresso e plano dessa task no harness Utilize modo planejamento e pensamento profundo. Inicie. toolUseResult.stdout: OlГЎ Claude! Precisamos criar uma versГЈo final da parte visual do app para apresentar nossa proposta ao cliente e ganharmos o processo de licitaГ§ГЈo. O mais importante Г© impressionarmos o cliente visualmente e interativamente com nosso frontend, de modo a demonstrar que podemos solucionar de forma excepcional as dores que ele reportou na RFQ (./docs/RFQ.md). Objetivo: Sua missГЈo Г© criar livremente um documento de UX/Design/Produto que irГЎ descrever a versГЈo final do produto. Este documento Г© para uso interno - nГЈo serГЎ apresentado aos clientes. A prГіxima tarefa serГЎ, atravГ©s deste documento, a implementaГ§ГЈo do frontend interativo com Astro.js e deck.gl, utilizando mocks para os dados. Portanto, o documento deve ser claro e completo com essa finalidade. Formato do documento: - Usar formato .md com o documento escrito em inglГӘs - Incluir um Moodboard com das referГӘncias reais (nГЈo apenas links) - Para cada decisГЈo de tela/soluГ§ГЈo, incluir imagens de exemplo de como deverГЎ ficar, usando screenshots de apps concorrentes ou outras referГӘncias Fontes e ReferГӘncias: - A fonte da verdade Г© exclusivamente a RFQ (docs/RFQ.md) - nГЈo considerar logicamente ou tecnicamente nenhum outro documento ou cГіdigo no projeto - Os demais documentos do projeto podem ser lidos apenas como complemento de pesquisa - acessar as fontes originais e verificar se cada dado se enquadra no novo planejamento - Fazer extensa pesquisa na internet para referГӘncias visuais e soluГ§Гөes ReferГӘncias de Design: - Usar o Linear como referГӘncia principal de design, especialmente a timeline - O "mood" do Linear (espaГ§amentos, cores, tipografia) serve de referГӘncia geral, mas nГЈo as soluГ§Гөes, por se tratar de problemas diferentes - Importante: Diferenciar claramente no documento o que Г© inspirado no Linear vs. o que Г© inspirado em apps de scheduling, manutenГ§ГЈo, ferrovias, etc. - para evitar confusГЈo durante as seГ§Гөes de implementaГ§ГЈo Tela Principal: - A tela mais importante Г© a de planejamento/visГЈo dos dados com a implementaГ§ГЈo do modo "sandbox" que servirГЎ para resolver a solicitaГ§ГЈo de "what-if scenarios" Tecnologia: - EstГЎ decidido e testado que o framework final serГЎ deck.gl - NГЈo implementaremos manualmente features que podem causar problemas cross-browser (zoom, pan, pitch, etc.) - devemos utilizar as melhores opГ§Гөes disponГӯveis para trabalhar com deck.gl Harness: - Utilizar o sistema harness para salvaguardar progresso em caso de necessidade de continuarmos em nova sessГЈo - Registrar o progresso e plano dessa task no harness Utilize modo planejamento e pensamento profundo. Inicie. The system 15вҶ’should offer an automated, data-driven, and real-time mileage management approach, considering 16вҶ’different preventive maintenance activities, current resources, and operational needs. mileage data. 24вҶ’в—ҸвҖӢ Ability to adjust the maintenance schedule based on available resources (man-hours), train 25вҶ’availability, and maintenance tracks. 26вҶ’в—ҸвҖӢ The software should be underrun or overrun, as they can impact overall 29вҶ’mileage management. 30вҶ’в—ҸвҖӢ Availability to perform simulations on various planning and scheduling options. 31вҶ’ 32вҶ’Operation Planning: Key requirement to fulfil the operational planning 33вҶ’в—ҸвҖӢ Recommend the train numbers to be used daily based on the fitness status and mileage. 34вҶ’в—ҸвҖӢ Recommend the run assignment (mileage and the network) for each train. 35вҶ’в—ҸвҖӢ Provide warnings and recommendations to correct or adjust fleet mileage by comparing the 36вҶ’predicted mileage with the actual mileage. 37вҶ’в—ҸвҖӢ Provide an content.content: OlГЎ Claude! Precisamos criar uma versГЈo final da parte visual do app para apresentar nossa proposta ao cliente e ganharmos o processo de licitacao. O mais importante Г© impressionarmos o cliente visualmente e interativamente com nosso frontend, de modo a demonstrar que podemos solucionar de forma exceptional as dores que ele reportou na RFQ (./docs/RFQ.md). Sua missГЈo Г© livremente criar um documento de UX/Design/Produto que irГЎ descrever a versГЈo final do produto, incluindo no documento imagens de referencia - usaremos .md e vocГӘ deve incluir um Moodboard com das referencias reais, o mesmo vale para as decisГөes de como serГЈo todas as outras solucoes, como vocГӘ nГЈo irГЎ desenhar realmente nesta tarefa, vocГӘ incluirГЎ imagens de exemplo de como deverГЎ ficar a tela, as imagens podem ser de telas de apps concorrentes ou outras referencias (usar , nГЈo incluir apenas links) - A fonte da verdade Г© a RFQ e, embora vocГӘ possa ler os demais documentos no projeto, deve considerar que muita coisa neles nГЈo ficou boa durante as POCs e precisamos de um plano final - estГЎ decidido e testado que o framework final serГЎ mesmo o deck.gl e que nГЈo implementaremos manualmente features que podem causar problemas cross-browser, como zoom, pan, pitch, etc - devemos utilizar as melhores opcoes disponiveis para se trabalhar com deck.gl dentro da nossa necessidade - vocГӘ deve buscar na internet, fazer extensa pesquisa e utilizar o sistema harness para savaguardar em caso de necessidade de continuarmos em nova sessГЈo. todos.content: Analyze Claude Code conversation logs from Dec 19-20
Identify what was done correctly vs hallucinations
Determine current state of DESIGN-REVISION task
Save analysis to harness
Execute DESIGN-REVISION task 1: Correct tech stack in DESIGN_SPEC.md
Execute DESIGN-REVISION task 2: Fetch new reference screenshots

content.thinking: O usuГЎrio quer que eu use jq para extrair as mensagens do usuГЎrio dos arquivos JSON. Vou fazer isso de forma mais eficiente.

input.command:
for f in ~/.claude/projects/-Users-anotonio-silva-antonio-mms-deck/*.jsonl; do
  echo "=== $(basename "$f") ==="
  jq -r 'select(.type == "user") | .message.content | if type == "string" then . else empty end' "$f" 2>/dev/null | head -500
done 2>/dev/null | grep -v "^\[" | grep -v "^===" | grep -v "^$" | head -300

content.content: OlГЎ Claude! Precisamos criar uma versГЈo final da parte visual do app para apresentar nossa proposta ao cliente e ganharmos o processo de licitaГ§ГЈo. O mais importante Г© impressionarmos o cliente visualmente e interativamente com nosso frontend, de modo a demonstrar que podemos solucionar de forma excepcional as dores que ele reportou na RFQ (./docs/RFQ.md). Objetivo: Sua missГЈo Г© criar livremente um documento de UX/Design/Produto que irГЎ descrever a versГЈo final do produto. Este documento Г© para uso interno - nГЈo serГЎ apresentado aos clientes. A prГіxima tarefa serГЎ, atravГ©s deste documento, a implementaГ§ГЈo do frontend interativo com Astro.js e deck.gl, utilizando mocks para os dados. Portanto, o documento deve ser claro e completo com essa finalidade. Formato do documento: - Usar formato .md com o documento escrito em inglГӘs - Incluir um Moodboard com das referГӘncias reais (nГЈo apenas links) - Para cada decisГЈo de tela/soluГ§ГЈo, incluir imagens de exemplo de como deverГЎ ficar, usando screenshots de apps concorrentes ou outras referГӘncias Fontes e ReferГӘncias: - A fonte da verdade Г© exclusivamente a RFQ (docs/RFQ.md) - nГЈo considerar logicamente ou tecnicamente nenhum outro documento ou cГіdigo no projeto - Os demais documentos do projeto podem ser lidos apenas como complemento de pesquisa - acessar as fontes originais e verificar se cada dado se enquadra no novo planejamento - Fazer extensa pesquisa na internet para referГӘncias visuais e soluГ§Гөes ReferГӘncias de Design: - Usar o Linear como referГӘncia principal de design, especialmente a timeline - O "mood" do Linear (espaГ§amentos, cores, tipografia) serve de referГӘncia geral, mas nГЈo as soluГ§Гөes, por se tratar de problemas diferentes - Importante: Diferenciar claramente no documento o que Г© inspirado no Linear vs. o que Г© inspirado em apps de scheduling, manutenГ§ГЈo, ferrovias, etc. - para evitar confusГЈo durante as seГ§Гөes de implementaГ§ГЈo Tela Principal: - A tela mais importante Г© a de planejamento/visГЈo dos dados com a implementaГ§ГЈo do modo "sandbox" que servirГЎ para resolver a solicitaГ§ГЈo de "what-if scenarios" Tecnologia: - EstГЎ decidido e testado que o framework final serГЎ deck.gl - NГЈo implementaremos manualmente features que podem causar problemas cross-browser (zoom, pan, pitch, etc.) - devemos utilizar as melhores opГ§Гөes disponГӯveis para trabalhar com deck.gl Harness: - Utilizar o sistema harness para salvaguardar progresso em caso de necessidade de continuarmos em nova sessГЈo - Registrar o progresso e plano dessa task no harness Utilize modo planejamento e pensamento profundo. Inicie. Eu tambГ©m gostei da densidade com claresa dessa imagem, que se parece tambГ©m com a nova interface do Intellij (islands) - imagino que para aplicaГ§Гөes complexas isso deva ser levado em consideraГ§ГЈo: https://camo.githubusercontent.com/8306f5c7cd78b81b7c87e1e0fff39a0f21166a572430f4712a4f8df0e0067e84/68747470733a2f2f7777772e6769746b72616b656e2e636f6d2f77702d636f6e74656e742f75706c6f6164732f323032322f30332f696e6465782d7468756d626e61696c2e706e67 - https://blog.jetbrains.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/image-33.png https://blog.jetbrains.com/fleet/2024/12/introducing-fleets-new-islands-ui/ Lembre-se que o design deve considerar o ambiente corporativo, deve ser botito, impressionante, mas nГЈo parecer amador de forma nenhuma. This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The conversation is summarized below: Analysis: Let me analyze the conversation chronologically: 1. **Initial Request (Portuguese)**: User wants to create a final visual version of the app to present to a client for a bidding process. The goal is to impress the client visually and interactively with the frontend, demonstrating solutions to pain points in the RFQ (docs/RFQ.md). 2. **Document Requirements**: - Format: .md in English - Include Moodboard with `` tags (actual images, not just links) - For each screen/solution, include example images from competitors or references - Source of truth: ONLY RFQ.md - Use Linear as main design reference - Differentiate clearly between Linear-inspired vs scheduling/maintenance/railway-inspired elements - Main screen: Planning view with "sandbox" mode for what-if scenarios - Technology: deck.gl (decided) - Use harness system for progress 3. **Files Read**: - docs/RFQ.md - Source of truth with pain points - claude-progress.txt - Previous session progress - features.json - Project features and tasks - docs/DESIGN_SPEC.md - Existing design document 4. **Plan Mode**: I entered plan mode and created a comprehensive plan at `/Users/anotonio.silva/.claude/plans/elegant-sparking-blossom.md` 5. **User Decisions from Questions**: - Captured Linear screenshots (homepage-hero.png, plan-page.png, timeline-view.png) - Captured DHTMLX Gantt screenshots (gantt-main.png, gantt-dark-theme.png, gantt-blog-dark.png) - Captured Railnova screenshot (railnova-railfleet.png) - User provided additional Islands UI references from JetBrains Fleet and GitKraken - Downloaded Islands UI images (jetbrains-fleet-islands.png, gitkraken-interface.png) - Fetched Fleet Islands UI design principles 7. **Document Written**: Created comprehensive DESIGN_SPEC.md with: - Key Technical Concepts: - **deck.gl** - GPU-accelerated WebGL visualization library - **Semantic Zoom** - MACRO/MESO/MICRO views based on zoom level - **Sandbox Mode** - What-if scenario simulation (PRIMARY FEATURE from RFQ) - **Islands UI Pattern** - JetBrains Fleet's modular panel layout for complex apps - **Linear Design** - Dark mode, typography-first, whitespace - **XState 5.x** - State machines for viewport and sandbox - **Astro.js** - Static generation with islands architecture - **RBush** - Spatial indexing for viewport culling - **@use-gesture/react** - Cross-platform input handling 3. Files and Code Sections: - **docs/RFQ.md** - Source of truth - 128 trains (49 Kinki Sharyo + 79 Alstom) - 12-year contract period (2024-2036) - 9 pain points including "No predictive simulations" - Maintenance types: A-Check, B-Check, C-Check, D-Overhaul - **docs/DESIGN_SPEC.md** - Main output (replaced entirely) - 867 lines of comprehensive design specification - Includes moodboard with local images - Design system with colors, typography, spacing - Application shell with Islands UI pattern - Planning View with semantic zoom - Sandbox Mode as PRIMARY FEATURE - Component specifications, data structures, interactions - **design/references/** - Downloaded images folder: - `deckgl/`: kepler-gl.jpg, flowmap.jpg, sanddance.jpg, avs.jpg - `linear/`: linear-homepage-hero.png, linear-plan-page.png, linear-timeline-view.png - `scheduling/`: dhtmlx-gantt-main.png, dhtmlx-gantt-dark-theme.png, dhtmlx-gantt-blog-dark.png - `railway/`: railnova-railfleet.png - `islands-ui/`: jetbrains-fleet-islands.png (495KB), gitkraken-interface.png (850KB) - **Plan file**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/.claude/plans/elegant-sparking-blossom.md` 4. Fixed by using relative filenames (e.g., `linear-homepage-hero.png`) which saves to `.playwright-mcp/` folder, then copying files to the correct location. 5.
